perturbation.original_text: 1.12 "UPDATE(S)" means any error corrections, bug fixes, modifications or enhancements to the Software, which are indicated by a change in the numeric identifier to the Software in the digit to the right of the decimal, or any error corrections, bug fixes, modifications or enhancements of the Software and MarketSite software used to operate the MarketSite.net Service.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 "UPDATE(S)" means error corrections to the Software.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'bug fixes, modifications or enhancements to the Software, which are indicated by a change in the numeric identifier to the Software in the digit to the right of the decimal, or any error corrections, bug fixes, modifications or enhancements of the Software and MarketSite software used to operate the MarketSite.net Service' from the definition of 'UPDATE(S),' the scope of what constitutes an update is narrowed. This contradicts Section 3.2, where Commerce One is obligated to provide pre-release versions of relevant Updates or Upgrades of the Software, because if updates only constitute error corrections, enhancements are not being provided and hence there is a breach. This change introduces uncertainty about what Commerce One is contractually obligated to provide as updates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 New Versions. Commerce One shall provide Corio with any pre-release versions of relevant Updates or Upgrades of the Software. Commerce One shall make these versions available to Corio to preview at the earliest possible date. Commerce One shall provide all such Updates and Upgrades to Corio free of additional charge and Corio shall, in its sole discretion determine when, and if, to offer any such Updates and/or Upgrades to its Customers.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'UPDATE(S)' broadly to include error corrections, bug fixes, modifications, or enhancements. The changed text narrows the definition to only error corrections. This directly contradicts section 3.2, which obligates Commerce One to provide 'Updates or Upgrades' to Corio. By restricting 'UPDATE(S)' to only error corrections, Commerce One is no longer explicitly obligated to provide pre-release versions of modifications or enhancements, leading to a potential breach of contract and creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Software Support and Maintenance Fees. Corio shall pay to Commerce One an annual Software Maintenance and Support fee for the support services to be provided by Commerce One specified in Exhibit C attached hereto and made a part hereof, and Updates and Upgrades, according to the fees set forth in Exhibit B hereto. Payment terms of annual Software Maintenance and Support fees shall be as set forth in Exhibit B hereto. Maintenance and Support shall automatically continue during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, provided that Corio continues to pay the annual Maintenance and Support fees contained in Exhibit B, attached hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Software Support and Maintenance Fees. Corio shall pay to Commerce One an annual Software Maintenance and Support fee for the support services to be provided by Commerce One specified in Exhibit C attached hereto and made a part hereof, according to the fees set forth in Exhibit B hereto. Payment terms of annual Software Maintenance and Support fees shall be as set forth in Exhibit B hereto. Maintenance and Support shall automatically continue during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, provided that Corio continues to pay the annual Maintenance and Support fees contained in Exhibit B, attached hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'and Updates and Upgrades' from Section 4.2, the obligation to pay for Updates and Upgrades is removed from the description of Software Support and Maintenance Fees. This omission creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.9, which states that Updates and Upgrades are subject to the terms of this Agreement and are included in the Maintenance and Support fees payable by Corio. The contract now implies Corio should pay for maintenance without explicitly entitling them to updates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.9 New Products. Updates and Upgrades to the Software and the MarketSite.net Service and software are subject to the terms of this Agreement and are included in the Maintenance and Support fees payable by Corio. Commerce One agrees to license Corio to use future products and solutions offered by Commerce One according to the license fees and other terms and conditions as the parties may agree. These products and solutions may include, but are not limited to the following: e-commerce, and marketing and sales force automation solutions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.2 omits 'and Updates and Upgrades' from the description of Software Support and Maintenance Fees. This directly contradicts Section 2.9, which explicitly states that 'Updates and Upgrades to the Software... are included in the Maintenance and Support fees payable by Corio.' By removing the reference to Updates and Upgrades in Section 4.2, while Section 2.9 still includes it in the maintenance fees, creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding what Corio is entitled to for the Maintenance and Support fees they are paying.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 By Commerce One. Commerce One shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Corio and its Customers from any and all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred by Corio or its Customers arising out of any claim that the Software infringes any patent, copyright, trademark or trade right secret of a third party; provided that Corio or its Customer promptly notifies Commerce One in writing of any such claim and promptly tenders the control and the defense and settlement of any such claim to Commerce One at Commerce One's expense and with Commerce One's choice of counsel. Corio or its Customer shall cooperate with Commerce One, at Commerce One's expense, in defending or settling such claim and Corio or its Customer may join in defense with counsel of its choice at its own expense. If the Software is, or in the opinion of Commerce One may become, the subject of any claim of infringement or if it is adjudicatively determined that the Software infringes, then Commerce One may, at its sole option and expense, either (i) procure for Corio the right from such third party to use the Software, (ii) replace or modify the Software with other suitable and substantially equivalent products so that the Software becomes noninfringing, or if (i) and (ii) are not practicable after Commerce One has exhausted all diligent efforts, (iii) terminate this Agreement and refund to Corio a pro-rated portion of the fees paid hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 By Commerce One. Commerce One shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Corio and its Customers from any and all damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) incurred by Corio or its Customers arising out of any claim related to the Software; provided that Corio or its Customer promptly notifies Commerce One in writing of any such claim and promptly tenders the control and the defense and settlement of any such claim to Commerce One at Commerce One's expense and with Commerce One's choice of counsel. Corio or its Customer shall cooperate with Commerce One, at Commerce One's expense, in defending or settling such claim and Corio or its Customer may join in defense with counsel of its choice at its own expense. If the Software is, or in the opinion of Commerce One may become, the subject of any claim or if it is adjudicatively determined, then Commerce One may, at its sole option and expense, either (i) procure for Corio the right from such third party to use the Software, (ii) replace or modify the Software with other suitable and substantially equivalent products , or if (i) and (ii) are not practicable after Commerce One has exhausted all diligent efforts, (iii) terminate this Agreement and refund to Corio a pro-rated portion of the fees paid hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'infringes any patent, copyright, trademark or trade right secret of a third party' in Section 8.1 broadens the scope of indemnity. This contradiction makes unclear and creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7.2, Intellectual Property Warranty, wherein Commerce One warrants, they are the sole owner of the Software and the software does not infringe. The new indemnity implies Commerce One must indemnify for any claim related to the software not just infringement claims, so that section 7.2 becomes meaningless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Intellectual Property Warranty. Commerce One represents and warrants to Corio that (a) Commerce One is the sole and exclusive owner of the Software; (b) Commerce One has full and sufficient right, title and authority to grant the rights and/or licenses granted to Corio under this Agreement; (c) the Software does not contain any materials developed by a third party used by Commerce One except pursuant to a license agreement; and (d) the Software does not infringe any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark or other intellectual property rights of a third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8.1 limited Commerce One's indemnity obligation to claims of intellectual property infringement. The changed text broadens this to 'any claim related to the Software.' This creates a direct conflict with Section 7.2, which specifically warrants that the software does not infringe any intellectual property rights. If Section 8.1 now covers 'any claim,' it contradicts the specific warranty in 7.2, making the scope of Commerce One's liability unclear and directly impacting the enforceability and meaning of Section 7.2.
